idea.hook: How many Solar Systems fit inside ONE Pillar of Creation?
facts: Our Solar System, from Sun to Neptune's orbit, spans about 4.1 light-hours (approx. 4.5 billion km).
The tallest 'Pillar of Creation' in the Eagle Nebula is roughly 4 light-years long.
One light-year is equivalent to 8,766 light-hours.
idea.payoff: You could line up over 8,500 entire Solar Systems, end-to-end, inside that single pillar!
idea.title: Pillars of Creation: YOU WON'T BELIEVE THE SIZE! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: How many Solar Systems fit inside ONE Pillar of Creation?
scenes.visual: A visually striking question mark overlayed on a dark, swirling nebula background with faint stars.

scenes.text: Our Solar System (Sun to Neptune's orbit) is ~4.1 light-hours wide.
scenes.visual: A stylized animation of our Solar System, with the Sun and planets visible, and an arc showing the diameter from the Sun to Neptune's orbit. Text appears above the system.

scenes.text: The tallest 'Pillar of Creation' in the Eagle Nebula is roughly 4 light-years long.
scenes.visual: A stunning, detailed image or animation of the tallest Pillar of Creation, showcasing its impressive length. A glowing line indicates its extent.
A clean infographic showing a conversion: '1 LY' on the left transforms into '8,766 LH' on the right with an arrow in between. Background remains space-themed.

scenes.text: You could line up OVER 8,500 Solar Systems end-to-end inside that SINGLE pillar!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, wide shot of the Pillars of Creation. A long line of tiny, glowing dots (representing Solar Systems) appears and stretches along the length of the tallest pillar. The number '8,500+' flashes brightly.

script.thumbnail_text: Pillars vs. Solar Systems: The True Scale!
